subject: computer-science
description: Cpu Memory Storage Devices MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: Which part of the computer is considered as the brain of the computer?
mcqs.explanation: The CPU (Central Processing Unit) is considered the brain of the computer — it performs all data processing operations and controls all other parts.

mcqs.q: How many parts does the CPU consist of?
mcqs.explanation: The CPU consists of three parts: Memory Unit, Control Unit and Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU).

mcqs.q: Which of the following is the FIRST part of the CPU?
mcqs.explanation: The Memory Unit is the first part of the CPU — it stores instructions, data and intermediate results.

mcqs.q: Which part of the CPU stores instructions, data and intermediate results?
mcqs.explanation: The Memory Unit (1st part of the CPU) stores instructions, data and intermediate results during processing.

mcqs.q: Which part of the CPU controls the operations of all parts of the computer but does NOT carry out actual data processing?
mcqs.explanation: The Control Unit (2nd part of CPU) controls all parts of the computer but does NOT carry out the actual data processing operations.

mcqs.q: Which part of the CPU is a digital circuit used to perform arithmetic and logic operations?
mcqs.explanation: The Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) is the digital circuit that performs arithmetic (add, subtract) and logic (AND, OR, NOT) operations.

mcqs.q: ALU stands for:
mcqs.explanation: ALU stands for Arithmetic Logic Unit — the third part of the CPU.

mcqs.q: Which component gives control of the CPU to the process selected by the short-term scheduler?
mcqs.explanation: The Dispatcher gives control of the CPU to the process selected by the short-term scheduler.

mcqs.q: In Priority Scheduling, the CPU is allocated to:
options: The process with the lowest priority
The process with the highest priority
mcqs.explanation: In the Priority Scheduling Algorithm, the CPU is allocated to the process with the highest priority.

mcqs.q: The CPU fetches an instruction from memory according to the value of:
mcqs.explanation: The CPU fetches the instruction from memory according to the value of the Programme Counter.

mcqs.q: The address generated by the CPU is called:
mcqs.explanation: The address generated by the CPU is called the Logical Address (also known as virtual address).

mcqs.q: A modern digital computer's central processor consists of:
mcqs.explanation: A modern digital computer's central processor consists of the control unit and primary memory.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT a part of the CPU?
mcqs.explanation: The Hard Disk Drive (HDD) is a secondary storage device — NOT a part of the CPU. The three CPU parts are MU, CU and ALU.

mcqs.q: The CPU performs which of the following primary functions?
options: All types of data processing operations and controls all parts
mcqs.explanation: The CPU performs all types of data processing operations and controls the operation of all parts of the computer.

mcqs.q: Which of the following CPU parts performs addition, subtraction, AND, OR operations?
mcqs.explanation: The Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) performs all arithmetic operations (add, subtract) and logic operations (AND, OR, NOT).

mcqs.q: What is a 'Cache HIT'?
options: When data is NOT found in the cache
When data is found in the cache memory
mcqs.explanation: A Cache HIT occurs whenever the requested data is found in the cache memory — improving CPU speed.

mcqs.q: In the context of CPU operation, which of the following is true about the Control Unit?
options: It controls all parts but doesn't carry out actual data processing
It is the same as ALU
mcqs.explanation: The Control Unit controls the operations of all parts of the computer but does NOT carry out actual data processing — that is done by the ALU.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is responsible for fetching, decoding and executing instructions?
mcqs.explanation: The CPU is responsible for fetching, decoding and executing instructions — making it the brain of the computer.

mcqs.q: Which CPU part stores intermediate results during a calculation?
mcqs.explanation: The Memory Unit (the first part of the CPU) stores instructions, data and intermediate results during processing.

mcqs.q: The CPU is also known as the:
mcqs.explanation: The CPU is known as the brain of the computer because it performs all data processing and controls all other components.

mcqs.q: What is computer memory?
options: An area where data and instructions are stored
mcqs.explanation: Computer memory is the area inside a computer (and auxiliary storage) where data and instructions are stored.

mcqs.q: What are the two major chips in a computer?
options: Primary memory chip and microprocessor chip
mcqs.explanation: The two major chips in a computer are the primary memory chip and the microprocessor chip.

mcqs.q: Primary memory holds:
options: Only those data and instructions on which the computer is currently working
mcqs.explanation: Primary memory holds only those data and instructions on which the computer is currently working.

mcqs.q: Secondary memory is used for:
mcqs.explanation: Secondary memory is used for storing data and information permanently — even after the computer is switched off.

mcqs.q: What are the two types of main memory?
mcqs.explanation: The two types of main memory are RAM (Random-Access Memory) and ROM (Read Only Memory).

mcqs.q: RAM stands for:
mcqs.explanation: RAM stands for Random-Access Memory — the volatile primary memory of the CPU.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is a VOLATILE memory?
mcqs.explanation: RAM is volatile — its contents are lost when the power is switched off.

mcqs.q: RAM is used to store:
options: Data, programme, and programme result currently in use
mcqs.explanation: RAM is the internal memory of the CPU used for storing data, programme, and programme result currently in use.

mcqs.q: RAM is what type of memory?
mcqs.explanation: RAM is a read/write memory — data can both be read from and written to it.

mcqs.q: ROM stands for:
mcqs.explanation: ROM stands for Read Only Memory — non-volatile memory whose contents cannot be altered.

mcqs.q: ROM is a:
mcqs.explanation: ROM is non-volatile — its contents are retained even when the power is switched off.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is TRUE about ROM?
options: It loses data on power off
mcqs.explanation: ROM is read-only memory — its contents cannot be altered. It is also non-volatile.

mcqs.q: Cache memory is a:
mcqs.explanation: Cache memory is a very high-speed semiconductor memory used to speed up the CPU.

mcqs.q: Cache memory is implemented using which type of chips?
mcqs.explanation: Cache memory is implemented using SRAM (Static RAM) chips because they are faster than DRAM.

mcqs.q: When data is found in the cache memory, it is called:
mcqs.explanation: When data is found in the cache memory it is called a Cache HIT — opposite of a Cache MISS.

mcqs.q: LRU stands for:
mcqs.explanation: LRU stands for Least Recently Used — a cache replacement policy that discards the least recently accessed entry.

mcqs.q: LRU is a type of:
mcqs.explanation: LRU (Least Recently Used) is a type of replacement policy used by cache memory.

mcqs.q: What is the efficient method for updating the cache called?
mcqs.explanation: Snoopy Writes is the efficient method for updating the cache — used in multiprocessor systems for cache coherence.

mcqs.q: FIFO is a:
mcqs.explanation: FIFO (First In First Out) is a page replacement algorithm — the oldest entry in memory is replaced first.

mcqs.q: A buffer in a computer is:
options: A temporary storage place for information
mcqs.explanation: A buffer is a temporary storage place for information in a computer — typically used to handle differences in data flow rates.

mcqs.q: Access Time refers to:
options: The time required for a computer to locate and transfer data
mcqs.explanation: Access Time is the time required for a computer to locate and transfer data from a storage device or memory.

mcqs.q: A chip in a computer is:
options: An extremely small piece of silicon on which integrated circuits are fabricated
mcqs.explanation: A chip is an extremely small piece of silicon on which integrated circuits (ICs) are fabricated.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is the internal memory of the CPU?
mcqs.explanation: RAM is the internal memory of the CPU used for storing data, programmes and results currently being processed.

mcqs.q: Which memory loses its data when the power is switched off?
mcqs.explanation: RAM is volatile — it loses all its data when the power is switched off, unlike ROM and storage devices.

mcqs.q: When a memory page containing a heavily used variable is removed using which algorithm, performance suffers?
mcqs.explanation: FIFO can remove a heavily used page if it was loaded early — this is a known weakness of FIFO compared to LRU.

mcqs.q: HDD stands for:
mcqs.explanation: HDD stands for Hard Disk Drive — a non-volatile magnetic storage device with spinning platters.

mcqs.q: Which of the following best describes an HDD?
mcqs.explanation: An HDD (Hard Disk Drive) is a non-volatile magnetic storage device capable of remembering vast amounts of data.

mcqs.q: SSD stands for:
mcqs.explanation: SSD stands for Solid State Drive — a non-volatile storage device with no moving parts, faster than HDD.

mcqs.q: Which of the following are examples of OPTICAL storage devices?
mcqs.explanation: CD, DVD and Blu-ray drives are optical storage devices — they use laser light to read and write data.

mcqs.q: Flash storage uses which type of memory to store data?
mcqs.explanation: Flash storage devices are non-volatile solid-state storage that use NAND flash memories (millions of transistors) to store data.

mcqs.q: A Flash Drive is also commonly called a:
mcqs.explanation: A Flash Drive is also commonly called a Pen Drive — it enables easy transport of data between computers.

mcqs.q: Bernoulli Disks are a type of:
mcqs.explanation: Bernoulli Disks are a type of magnetic hard disk.

mcqs.q: What is a Disk Array?
options: A set of hard disk drives with a controller mounted in a single box
mcqs.explanation: A Disk Array is a set of hard disk drives with a controller mounted in a single box — forming a single large storage unit.

mcqs.q: The process in which a file is partitioned into smaller parts and different parts are stored in different disks is called:
mcqs.explanation: Stripping is the process where a file is partitioned into smaller parts and stored across multiple disks for performance.

mcqs.q: Disk Packs use:
options: A number of hard disk platters
mcqs.explanation: Disk Packs use a number of hard disk platters stacked together to provide larger storage capacity.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT a type of optical disk?
mcqs.explanation: A Winchester Disk is NOT an optical disk — it is a type of magnetic hard disk.

mcqs.q: A Memory Card serves a similar purpose to a:
mcqs.explanation: A Memory Card serves a similar purpose to a Flash Drive (Pen Drive) — both are non-volatile portable solid-state storage.

mcqs.q: Storage devices are computer hardware used to:
mcqs.explanation: Storage devices are computer hardware used to remember and store data permanently or temporarily.

mcqs.q: Which of the following storage devices has no moving parts?
mcqs.explanation: SSD (Solid State Drive) has no moving parts — it uses solid-state flash memory, making it faster and more reliable than HDDs.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is TRUE about flash drives?
options: They use NAND flash memory and are non-volatile
They are slower than floppy disks
mcqs.explanation: Flash drives are non-volatile solid-state storage devices that use NAND flash memory — they retain data without power.
